parts.text:
Found 1 blocking issue.

- `runWorkspaceDeploymentInlineWithRetries` still has a `running -> queued` race under cancel-requested, so the regression is not fully fixed for the inline path. In `recoverInlineRunningDeployment`, it checks `latest.cancelRequestedAt` first, then does an unconditional `updateWorkspaceDeploymentStatus(..., 'queued', ...)` (`packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deployment-runner.ts:2047`, `packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deployment-runner.ts:2055`). If cancel is requested between those two steps, inline recovery can still flip `running` to `queued` with `cancel_requested_at` set; on the next pass `processWorkspaceDeployment` immediately cancels from queued (`packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deployment-runner.ts:1520`) instead of reconciling active provider state. This is exactly the class of regression the queue-path SQL guard fixed (`packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deployment-runner.ts:1829` with `cancel_requested_at IS NULL`), but inline does not have the same atomic protection.

- Tests do not cover this inline race/fix parity. Current new coverage validates queue-path protection and cancel-requested handling, but there is no inline test asserting that retry recovery cannot transition `running` to `queued` once cancel is requested concurrently (`packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deployment-runner.test.ts`). This leaves the blocking bug above unguarded.
